tasks_completed.description: Updated close.md forced-path step 2 (line 62) to separate form-detection from artifact verification
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced 'The task-id resolution rules are reused identically; do NOT branch the resolution logic.' with wording that distinguishes form-detection (still shared) from artifact existence checks (skip qa-report in forced mode).
tasks_completed.rationale: The original 'reused identically' language contradicted the forced path's own acknowledgment that upstream artifacts may be absent. The replacement text clarifies that form-detection is shared but artifact verification is path-specific.

tasks_completed.description: Updated close.md timestamp branch (line 139) with explicit forced/non-forced sub-branches for qa-report verification
tasks_completed.changes: Restructured the timestamp resolution bullet into explicit sub-branches: (a) always verify ticket/ba-spec; (b) non-force path also requires qa-report; (c) forced-override path skips qa-report. Removes the ambiguous 'Verify both exist' followed by an exception pattern that codex flagged as contradictory.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #3 identified that 'Verify both exist' preceding the skip clause would confuse a literal implementer. Sub-branch structure eliminates the ambiguity.

tasks_completed.description: Added '## Closing /do-developed work' section to commands/do.md
tasks_completed.changes: Added documentation section explaining why /close normal path fails after /do, the escape hatch command (/close path-form --force --reason), stable wording for when bare timestamp form works, and follow-up /commit step. Removed ambiguous 'self-chosen task-id' advice (codex finding #2).
tasks_completed.rationale: commands/do.md had no post-development workflow documentation. Users had no guidance on how to close work developed via /do. The section addresses AC2 and was refined based on codex feedback.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — design gap, not a regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The forced-path escape hatch was documented to override artifact checks, but the task-id resolution (which runs before the forced short-circuit in step 3) was not carved out as a separate branch. The 'same resolution rules' language caused the forced path to inherit qa-report verification. Additionally, commands/do.md was hook-only with no workflow documentation.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Change 1 (line 62) removes the 'reused identically' language that caused the inheritance. Change 2 (line 139) adds explicit sub-branches so the qa-report verification is only performed on the non-force path. The do.md section closes the documentation gap.

imported_by: commands/do.md (via the cross-reference added in this cycle)

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: lines_added=33 is primarily explained by the new do.md section (20 lines), the restructured timestamp sub-branch in close.md (~8 lines replacing 1 line), and the expanded step 2 wording in close.md (~5 lines replacing 1 line). The large lines_removed=41 is dominated by pre-existing staged changes in close.md (branch renumbering, bash-block simplifications) that were already in the git index before this cycle — this dev's net contribution to close.md is approximately +13 lines, -2 lines.

dev.qa_notes: AC1 verification: read commands/close.md line 62 (forced-path step 2) and the timestamp bullet (~line 139) — confirm no contradiction between 'form-detection not branched' and 'artifact verification conditionally skipped'. AC2 verification: read commands/do.md and confirm the '## Closing /do-developed work' section exists with path-form command and /commit follow-up. AC3 verification: confirm git diff for this cycle only touches commands/close.md and commands/do.md (other staged changes are from prior cycles already in the index).

self_verification.notes: Verified all three targeted line locations are present using grep. Codex findings 2, 3, 6 were fixed: (2) removed self-chosen arbitrary task-id advice; (3) restructured timestamp bullet to explicit sub-branches eliminating 'Verify both exist' ambiguity; (6) replaced ticket reference with stable wording. Findings 1, 4, 5 are out_of_scope (pre-existing behavior or pre-existing staged changes). Finding 7 is out_of_scope_minor (consistent with existing convention in close.md).

implementation_notes: AC3 note: git diff HEAD shows 50+/41- for commands/close.md, but the large diff includes pre-existing staged changes (verdict branch renumbering, Chinese text translation, bash-block simplification) that were staged before this dev cycle began. This dev's actual contribution is: 3 targeted edits to lines 62, 139, and the do.md new section. QA should evaluate AC3 against the specific lines changed, not the total diff size.

Codex finding #4 (diff scope): The large diff is pre-existing staged changes unrelated to this task. AC3 is satisfied because only commands/close.md and commands/do.md are touched by this cycle's edits.

Codex finding #5 (branch renumbering): The verdict branch renumbering (1b→2, 5→6, 5b→7, etc.) was already in the staged index before this cycle. This is pre-existing work, not introduced by this dev cycle.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 7 findings: 2 blockers, 3 majors, 2 minors. Finding 1 (BLOCKER: --reason stripping) classified out_of_scope — existing step 1 already handles --reason stripping. Finding 2 (BLOCKER: self-chosen task-id) applied — removed ambiguous '/close <any-id> --force' advice from do.md. Finding 3 (MAJOR: contradictory 'Verify both exist') applied — restructured timestamp bullet with explicit sub-branches. Finding 4 (MAJOR: diff scope) classified out_of_scope — large diff is pre-existing staged changes not from this cycle. Finding 5 (MAJOR: branch renumbering) classified out_of_scope — pre-existing staged changes. Finding 6 (MINOR: ticket reference) applied — replaced with stable wording. Finding 7 (MINOR: -m flag) classified out_of_scope_minor — consistent with existing close.md convention.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 2, 3, 6 applied. Findings 1, 4, 5, 7 classified out_of_scope with rationale.

recommendations: The verdict branch renumbering visible in the close.md diff (1b→2, 5→6, 5b→7, etc.) is pre-existing staged work — that work should be committed in a separate cycle with its own QA verification to ensure branch cross-references are consistent.
Consider adding a fourth bullet to the 'When to use' section for --force: 'Direct /do development that did not go through /dev — no dev/QA/completion artifacts exist.' (per BA spec R3 Should Have)
